missed_changes: Strong embrace of OPTIMISTIC rollups and the Optimism/OP Stack ecosystem specifically — built and championed the Magi OP Stack client and repeatedly boosted Optimism (🟠), plus EIP-4844/proto-danksharding and the broad L2 landscape; the prediction had framed scaling chiefly through ZK rollups.
Broad AGI/general-AI commentary beyond the AI×crypto thesis (AGI-timeline takes like 'AGI before Mars', 'machines from toys to gods', stacking-sigmoids model of progress).

Vocal open-source-AI advocacy not in the prediction: criticizing OpenAI's increasingly 'equivocating' closed models and Meta's restricted LLaMA release, arguing weights should be public ('open source is the only way to preserve the flame').
New policy/geography concern: the U.S. losing its web3 developer lead and needing regulatory clarity, plus the idea that well-designed protocol rules can reduce the need for regulation.
New emphasis on formal verification / smart-contract security tooling (Halmos symbolic testing, Auction Zoo, etc.) and client-diversity advocacy.
notes: Almost the entire excited cluster is directly acted on in the window: ZK (KZG ceremony, zkDocs, zkdrops, Lasso/Jolt, danksharding, ZK report slide), on-chain games (CCP/EVE seed, mutating-state/composability discussion), AI×crypto (the '0-person unicorn: autonomous program on a crypto network that funds and grows itself'), decentralized social (Farcaster, xmtp, the 6/30 closed-corporate-network thread), NFTs/creator economy (royalties vs Meta, patronage/open editions, NFTs-as-programs), and DeFi/composability (State of Crypto, element_fi). The 1/26 token-design talk is a textbook confirmation of the anti-'pie-chart tokenomics' concern ('the token is not the protocol'; 'token-first thinking' as a failure pattern) and of the MKR risk-token thesis (MKR dilutes holders to cover shortfalls = underwriting risk). ETH and the risk-on/price-innovation-cycle stance are confirmed by the State of Crypto report and podcast. Decentralized identity is the softest 'confirmed' — supported indirectly via zkDocs (verifiable credentials/control of data) and the 4/29 reply favoring individual-level (vs state) identity, rather than explicit SIWE/DID login talk. The two ABSENT items (CEX proof-of-reserves and CEX/BNB-token concern) simply never come up in the provided window — the FTX/Binance custody theme that dominated his Dec-2022 posts is not revisited here; this is non-address, not reversal. No contradictions found.
